name: Namur

overview.description: Namur is the capital of Wallonia, dominated by its hilltop Citadel at the confluence of the Sambre and Meuse rivers — one of the largest fortress complexes in Europe.
overview.foundedNote: Long a strategically important fortress city at the meeting point of the Sambre and Meuse rivers
whyVisit: The Citadel of Namur — 80 hectares of grounds with roughly 7km of underground tunnels, among the largest fortresses in Europe
A cable car (since 2021) linking the city centre to the Citadel across the Sambre river
Compact, walkable historic centre with Baroque architecture such as Saint-Loup church
Base for exploring the Wallonia countryside and the Meuse valley

attractions.name: Citadel of Namur
attractions.description: A vast hilltop fortress with ~7km of underground tunnels, guided tours, a tourist train, and a cable car connecting it to the city centre.

attractions.name: Saint-Loup Church
attractions.description: A Baroque church in the historic centre, considered one of the finest examples of Baroque art in the former southern Low Countries.

transportation.description: TEC buses cover the city and surrounding area.
Since 2021, a cable car connects the Esplanade de la Citadelle to the city centre across the Sambre river.

hotels.name: City Centre
hotels.notes: Walkable to the old town and within reach of the Citadel via cable car or on foot.

foodSpots.name: Restaurants in the historic centre
foodSpots.notes: Limited dedicated Indian dining options; best explored as a day trip combined with a meal in Brussels or Liège.

tips: The Citadel's underground tunnels and cable car make it worth a half-day visit on its own.
Namur is a good base for exploring the wider Wallonia countryside and the Meuse valley.
French is the primary language spoken in Namur.
